perturbation.original_text: The term “ Representatives ” shall include the directors, officers, employees, agents, partners or advisors (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers and financial advisors) of the Recipient or Provider, as applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “ Representatives ” shall include the directors, officers, staff, agents, partners or advisors (including, without limitation, attorneys, accountants, consultants, bankers and financial advisors) of the Recipient or Provider, as applicable.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'employees' to 'staff' within the definition of "Representatives" introduces another level of ambiguity. It creates uncertainty about whether the terms “employees” and “staff” are interchangeable throughout the document. This lack of clarity can have implications, especially if the term "employees" is used later in a context where it's crucial to determine if 'staff' is also included. For example in Section 7, "employees" is being used but because the term "Representatives" are undefined, there is uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Recipient agrees that, for eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, no person in its organization who receives or has access to the Evaluation Material or has knowledge about the Possible Transaction will, directly or indirectly, solicit, or cause the soliciting of, or assist or encourage others in the soliciting of, the employment or consulting services of any of the officers of a Provider or any of its subsidiaries or employees engaged in research and development for Provider or any of its subsidiaries or any other employee of the Provider or any if its subsidiaries with whom the Recipient has had contact in connection with its evaluation of a Possible Transaction or of whom it has become aware as a result of its receipt of any Evaluation Material, so long as they are employed by the Provider or any of its subsidiaries and for two months thereafter (each, a “ Covered Employee ”).
perturbation.justification: The change from 'employees' to 'staff' in the definition of 'Representatives' introduces ambiguity. Section 7 uses the term 'employees' and places obligations upon them. If 'staff' is not clearly included within 'Representatives,' it is unclear whether the obligations extend to 'staff,' creating a contradiction because the changed definition makes the scope of the term 'employees' in Section 7 uncertain, which is a strong in-text contradiction according to the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Each Recipient shall use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and, subject to Section 5 , will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Recipient’s Representatives for the purpose of helping the Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Recipient shall use the Evaluation Data solely for the purpose of evaluating a Possible Transaction and, subject to Section 5 , will not disclose any of the Evaluation Material in any manner whatsoever; provided, however , that any of such information may be disclosed to the Recipient’s Representatives for the purpose of helping the Recipient evaluate a Possible Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Evaluation Material' to 'Evaluation Data' creates a conflict. The document uses 'Evaluation Material' extensively, and it's defined in Section 2. By changing the term in Section 3, it's now unclear whether the restrictions that apply to 'Evaluation Material' also apply to 'Evaluation Data' or if the two terms have the same meaning. The contradicted location is section 6 because evaluation material is being referred, which is not the same to Evaluation Data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If either Party decides that it does not wish to proceed with a Possible Transaction, it will promptly inform the other Party of that decision. In that case, or at any time upon the request of a Provider for any reason, a Recipient will, promptly after receipt of such notice or request, destroy all Evaluation Material in any way relating to the Provider or its products, services, employees or other assets or liabilities, and no copy or extract thereof (including electronic copies) shall be retained. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (i) a Recipient shall not be required to delete, erase or destroy any Evaluation Material contained in an archived computer backup system stored as a result of automated back-up procedures, and (ii) a Recipient may retain one copy of the Evaluation Material in its Office of the General Counsel solely for record-keeping purposes. The Recipient shall provide to the Provider a certificate of compliance with this provision. Notwithstanding the destruction or retention of the Evaluation Material, the Recipient and its Representatives will continue to be bound by such Recipient’s obligations hereunder with respect to such Evaluation Material.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Evaluation Material' to 'Evaluation Data' in Section 3, while Section 2 defines 'Evaluation Material', creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text in Section 6. Section 6 refers specifically to 'Evaluation Material' and requires its destruction or retention under certain conditions. By introducing 'Evaluation Data' in Section 3, it becomes unclear whether the obligations in Section 6 apply to 'Evaluation Data' as well, leading to uncertainty and a potential contradiction. This misalignment of terminology, as per the definition, leads to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall be responsible for any violation of this Agreement by any of its Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term 'breach' to 'violation' creates ambiguity as the contract may imply a specific legal meaning for 'breach', and 'violation' is more generic. This creates confusion regarding the level of severity needed to trigger consequences under the agreement, and thus an in-text contradiction. Because the term being used to define what is considered a breach for the agreement is not the same, or defined, then it creates an in-text contradiction on the Section 12.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party understands and agrees that money damages may not be a sufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by either Party or any of its Representatives and that the Party against which such breach is committed shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunction and specific performance, as a remedy for any such breach or threat thereof. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach by either Party of this Agreement, but shall be in addition to all other remedies available at law or equity to the Party against which such breach is committed.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'breach' to 'violation' introduces ambiguity into the contract. The contradicted text specifically references 'breach' and the remedies available for a 'breach'. By changing the original text to 'violation', it is unclear whether 'violation' encompasses the same level of severity as 'breach', potentially conflicting with the remedies outlined in the contradicted text (Section 12), as those remedies are specifically tied to a 'breach'. This creates an in-text contradiction as the contract now uses two terms potentially interchangeably ('breach' in Section 12 and 'violation' elsewhere) without defining whether they are equivalent, leading to uncertainty about when the remedies described in the contradicted text are applicable, adhering to the Misaligned Terminology definition.
